prompt:
Bug: classic-laddie's auto-rebuild-on-push has never worked. cosmo's hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix defines a github-relay consumer (consumer 'cosmo-rebuild', action 'systemd', unit 'cosmo-rebuild') that should start cosmo-rebuild.service when cosmo main gets a push. The relay receives the push and attempts the dispatch, but it fails — journal evidence from classic-laddie:

  github-relay[3964]: ERROR 'dispatch failed' consumer=cosmo-rebuild action=systemd error='systemctl start cosmo-rebuild: exit status 1: Failed to start cosmo-rebuild.service: Access denied as the requested operation requires interactive authentication. However, interactive authentication has not been enabled by the calling program.'

Root cause: the github-relay service runs as an unprivileged user (inspect inputs.github-relay.nixosModules.default — the flake input's module source, or the registered local project at ~/hack/github-relay — to find out exactly how the service runs: DynamicUser? dedicated user?), and polkit denies org.freedesktop.systemd1 manage-units to non-root callers without interactive auth.

FIX in cosmo (hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix or a small module): grant the relay's service identity permission to start EXACTLY cosmo-rebuild.service — least privilege, not blanket manage-units. The NixOS-idiomatic option is a security.polkit.extraConfig rule matching action org.freedesktop.systemd1.manage-units, unit cosmo-rebuild.service, verb start, and the relay's user — BUT if the relay uses DynamicUser=true a polkit user match won't work (dynamic UIDs); in that case pick the cleanest alternative and justify it in the PR, e.g.: give the relay unit a static User=, or have the relay consumer trigger via a root-owned systemd.path/socket unit it can poke through a writable path, or add a narrowly-scoped sudoers entry (security.sudo.extraRules, NOPASSWD, exact command /run/current-system/sw/bin/systemctl start cosmo-rebuild.service) if the relay invokes systemctl via sudo — check what the relay binary actually execs before choosing; do not modify the github-relay repo itself, only cosmo.

ALSO fix in the same PR (same unit, cosmetic-but-real): journal shows 'Unknown key StartLimitIntervalSec in section [Service], ignoring' — cosmo puts StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst in serviceConfig but they are [Unit] keys; move them to unitConfig on systemd.services.cosmo-rebuild so the rate-limiting actually applies.

TESTS: nix flake check; nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath; additionally render and grep the generated unit/polkit files from the eval to prove the fix is in the closure (e.g. eval config.systemd.services.cosmo-rebuild.unitConfig, and the polkit/sudoers config text). State clearly in the PR body that the fix only takes effect after the NEXT successful rebuild, so the first deploy after merging needs a manual 'sudo systemctl start cosmo-rebuild' (or nixos-rebuild switch) on classic-laddie — after that, the loop is self-sustaining.
